projectDescription: A self-referential self-improving agent framework from Meta Research that uses a meta-agent to iteratively evolve task agents across diverse benchmark domains.
keyFeatures: Self-referential self-improvement: the meta-agent can modify its own code and agent architecture
Multi-domain evaluation across diverse benchmarks (search, games, robotics, math, coding)
Docker-based sandbox execution for safe evaluation of model-generated code
Archive-based parent selection with configurable strategies (score_prop, score_child_prop, best, random)
Staged evaluation pipeline to reduce compute costs
Ensemble scoring across multiple evolved agent generations
Support for multiple LLM backends via LiteLLM (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini)
Configurable optimization modes: agent-only, ensemble-only, or both
publicApiSurface: generate_loop() — main entry point for the evolutionary self-improvement loop
MetaAgent.forward(repo_path, eval_path, iterations_left) — runs the meta-agent to modify the codebase
TaskAgent.forward(inputs) — runs the task agent on a given domain input
AgentSystem — abstract base class for all agents
chat_with_agent(msg, model, msg_history, tools_available) — LLM chat loop with tool use
get_response_from_llm(msg, model, temperature, max_tokens, msg_history) — single LLM call via LiteLLM
harness(agent_path, domain, num_samples, num_workers) — domain evaluation harness
